programme.title: Alaskan Bush People

programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Seven Deadly Sins

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: SPCA staff and volunteers are called in to remove almost 200 birds and mammals from an animal sanctuary after allegations of neglect. An investigator is just in time to save four chihuahuas from starvation, while nine dogs and four cats are rescued from a filthy house

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigators are called in when a drugs raid also uncovers roosters kept for fighting, while Dana Moss rescues two dogs on the verge of giving birth

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood - Part Two: American Filmmaker
programme.desc: Part two of two. Profile of Clint Eastwood, examining his lifelong commitment to the process of film-making. The actor made his directorial debut with the 1971 thriller Play Misty for Me, which proved the foundation for a career encompassing classics such as The Outlaw Josey Wales, Bird and Unforgiven. With contributions from Gene Hackman, Meryl Streep and Dina Ruiz

programme.title: This Cultural Life
programme.desc: Director Ken Loach talks to John Wilson about the key moments in his life that helped shaped his creativity. From his Midlands childhood as the son of a factory worker and annual summer holidays in Blackpool to his love for Czech cinema, which would become a huge inspiration on his own career. He also discusses his films for television, including Up the Junction and Cathy Come Home

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Rugby Boys: The Final
programme.desc: Rugby players compete against a trio from the Cambridge Quiz Society in the final of the quiz show where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his epic exploration of the cosmos, beginning with the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe, the stars. Throughout the opening episode, he looks at how stars created life and meaning in the cosmos, from the dawn of time through to the arrival of our own star, the Sun

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure the last place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Three civil servants with a shared love of Radio 4's The Archers pit their wits against a trio of music lovers to make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren presents

programme.title: The Making of King Arthur
programme.desc: Poet Simon Armitage traces the evolution of the Arthurian legend through the literature of the medieval age, revealing that King Arthur is not the great national hero he is usually considered to be. He is a fickle and transitory character who was appropriated by the Normans to justify their conquest; he was cuckolded when French writers began adapting the story; and it took Thomas Malory's masterpiece of English literature, Le Mort d'Arthur, to restore his dignity and reclaim him as the national hero known today

programme.title: Unforgiven (1992)
programme.desc: Two notorious gunslingers come out of retirement to help a young assassin collect the $1,000 bounty on the heads of a pair of cowboys who attacked and mutilated a prostitute in the backwoods town of Big Whiskey. The local sheriff, however, has no intention of letting the pair impinge on his own authority. Oscar-winning Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, with Gene Hackman, Morgan Freeman, Jaimz Woolvett and Richard Harris

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood - Part One: Out of the West
programme.desc: Part one of two. Profile of Hollywood icon Clint Eastwood, focusing on his poverty-stricken childhood in Thirties California, through early television success in Rawhide, to classic roles in films, including A Fistful of Dollars. With contributions from luminaries, such as Rip Torn, Eli Wallach and directors Don Siegel, Sergio Leone and Martin Scorsese

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 17, 1999. Featuring Tom Jones and Cerys Matthews, B*Witched and Ladysmith Black Mambazo, Progress Presents The Boy Wunda, Vengaboys, Artful Dodger feat Craig David and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn looks at the weekly pop chart from 1977 and introduces Osibisa, the Wurzels, Neil Innes, Demis Roussos, Bob Marley and the Wailers, ELO, Frankie Miller, the Stranglers, Honky and Rod Stewart, with a dance sequence from Legs & Co

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Exploration of the cultural impact of the USA's spectacular landscape, revealing how although the country had no ruins or ancient monuments, natural wonders could be found almost anywhere. Presenter Robert Hughes shows how the rise of the railroad opened up a whole new world of discovery while at the same time inaugurating America's industrial future

programme.title: Tina Turner at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of performances from one of the greatest singers and live performers in modern music - the legendary Tina Turner. This selection of her biggest hits come from appearances Tina made on various BBC programmes over the years, and features some of her biggest hits from the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s

programme.title: When Tina Turner Came to Britain
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the singer's 50-year relationship with the UK, featuring interviews with Tina herself, and contributions from those who have worked with her. The film follows her from her first visit to Britain to support the Rolling Stones in 1966, to her 1980s solo reinvention, through to playing in front of huge stadium audiences across the UK and the world premiere of Tina: The Musical in London. Featuring contributions from Arlene Phillips, Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware of Heaven 17, PP Arnold and Skin of Skunk Anansie

programme.title: Summer of 76
programme.desc: The story of the Harris family, who were determined to go through with their summer holiday despite a series of misfortunes that befell them. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances

But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.sub-title: Is It a Bedroom, Or Is It a Garage?
programme.desc: The team help the Jamiesons from East Sussex transform the home that's been in their family for 40 years. They strip every item from the converted bungalow and display them all in a warehouse where they discover 106 animal ornaments, 87 board games and over 1,000 pieces of china. As they sort through the decades of keepsakes, mum Nadine reflects on her childhood and the challenges of raising her daughters

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: At Drylaw Police Station in Edinburgh, Inspector Alison Lawrie investigates when a call comes in reporting vulnerable 18-year-old Taylor missing. Officers visit his adoptive parents' house to find out whether they have seen him but he hasn't answered any calls or messages from anyone and their concern grows when they learn Taylor has recently started transitioning from female to male. In the south of the city, Sergeant McMinn is on duty when a report that 13-year-old Ava has failed to return to her care unit after school comes in and alarm bells ring when he discovers she has no phone on her

The two players have won the Ballon d'or 13 times between them, with Messi winning eight of them, and they both head to this World Cup for what is likely to be the final time

Former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows how even fancy apartments are targets for thieves. And Anabela is gobsmacked when she discovers her cat has been stolen, but will turning to social media manage to recover him?

She wants to establish holiday lets and also rehome dogs in need, so Sonali takes her to a sanctuary to find out what it takes to look after the thousands of rescue animals in their care

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Canada v Bosnia and Herzegovina (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the Group B match at Toronto Stadium. The co-hosts are competing at just their third World Cup finals, with previous appearances coming in 1986 and four years ago in Qatar, where they lost all three of their group matches against Morocco, Croatia and Belgium, meaning they have lost every game they have played at the tournament. While expectations may be low, Jesse Marsch's men will hope to take full advantage of playing on home soil and produce the type of performances they did in the 2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup, when they won two of their group matches to progress to the quarter-finals, before being eliminated by Guatemala on penalties. Bosnia and Herzegovina qualified for this tournament after coming through the UEFA qualification play-off round just three months ago, beating Wales in a penalty shootout following a 1-1 draw in Cardiff, which they then repeated on a memorable night in Zenica to reach this tournament for just the second time in their history, condemning Italy to a third consecutive absence at this event. With analysis from Olivier Giroud and Micah Richards

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Queen James
programme.desc: Author and historian Gareth Russell reveals the story of King James VI & I, building a picture of a man who oversaw the creation of the King James Bible, was patron to Shakespeare, stepped up the plantation of Ulster and narrowly escaped being blown up in the Gunpowder Plot. However, in court circles, James was well known for having his 'favourites'; a succession of young, good-looking men, who were welcomed into his inner sanctum as close friends, political advisors and, in many cases, bedfellows. These favoured associates would typically survive for a few years, before James would move on to a newer, younger model, with this story focusing on a sensational murder trial that threatened to shake the monarchy to its core. James McArdle reads extracts from James' letters, writings and speeches

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Joyce accidentally eats some chocolate with magic mushrooms in it. Tim comes in for an appointment and gets on really well with Bruce, much to Alex's dismay

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best travels along the Foyle river network, criss-crossing his way across the many rivers and streams that make up its great catchment

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: USA v Paraguay (Kick-off 2.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group D match at Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood. While the Americans were guaranteed a place in this tournament as one of the three co-hosts, Paraguay progressed from the CONMEBOL qualification process, and though they finished sixth in the standings, they were one of four teams to finish tied on 28 points and were just one point behind second-placed Ecuador. There have been nine previous meetings between these nations, the first of which saw the USA claim a 3-0 victory at the inaugural World Cup in Uruguay in 1930, and most recently they clashed in a friendly encounter in November last year, when the United States triumphed by a 2-1 scoreline. With analysis from Ashley Williams and Steph Houghton

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar's investigation reveals a white supremacist that was campaigning outside the Suites hotel ahead of the protest may have influenced the riot. He also heads further afield to Scampton, Lincolnshire where residents have been campaigning against the Home Office's planned asylum centre in the former RAF base

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asia's endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049 (2017)
programme.desc: An android-hunter working for the Los Angeles Police Department discovers a long-buried secret, which leads him to track down former detective Rick Deckard, who's been missing for 30 years and is linked to the case. Denis Villeneuve's sci-fi thriller, the sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 classic, starring Ryan Gosling, Harrison Ford, Ana de Armas and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar visits Kirkby, Merseyside to find out how a peaceful protest outside a hotel housing asylum seekers turned into a riot in February 2023 and uncovers the blueprint for a national wave of violent riots that took place across the country in the summer of 2024. Mobeen is soon confronted with serious allegations about what a group of asylum seekers have supposedly been up to in the small town, and attempts to uncover the truth but locals close ranks and warn each other against speaking to him, claiming Mobeen and the film crew will twist their words to make them look bad

programme.title: Planet Sex with Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara examines the idea of gender, what it is and why it matters, confronting her own gender fluidity and masculinity along the way. She explores drag with a trans man in LA, discovers a third gender culture in Mexico and experiences a 'gender swap' in Spain

programme.title: America's New Female Right
programme.desc: In America, a new wave of young women who are championing extreme views on social media, including content that is increasingly shocking and - surprisingly - antifeminist. Journalist Layla Wright travels from her home town in Liverpool to meet influencers at the forefront of a new movement on the political right who use highly inflammatory language

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: The search for the gang of thieves known for stalking celebrities on social media and targeting those with expensive watches. Journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones' £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asia's endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Timor-Leste in Southeast Asia in search of the blue whale, and also hopes to find a saltwater crocodile, one of the giants of land

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: At the launch of her highly anticipated skincare product, a wellness business owner suddenly dies from an allergic reaction. Mervin quickly deduces her death to be suspicious when, despite the presence of peanuts in her throat, the victim didn't eat or drink anything. Meanwhile, Darlene struggles to train up new officer Seb Rose as they investigate an obscure party drug that's doing the rounds on the island. Tensions run high as Selwyn battles devastating news, while Mervin begins to doubt his own instincts when he uncovers compelling evidence in his mother's case. Drama, starring Don Gilet and Don Warrington

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: Planet Sex with Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara sets out to discover if there are more ethical, alternative types of porn than the mainstream that she can feel good about. Working with director Erika Lust, who wants to change the porn industry for the better, Cara helps make an 'ethical' feminist porn film in Barcelona, while in Berlin, she visits the Schwules Museum - the first museum in the world dedicated to gay history

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Gemma and Lorcan throw a party, but Gemma finds one of her old personality quirks coming to the fore again as she struggles to maintain her identity amidst the intoxicating brew of Lorcan's old-fashioned country charm. Callum makes a new friend in Gemma's brother David, but finds there's a catch. Comedy, with Rian Lennon, Ryan Dylan and Ele McKenzie

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna tries to find a boyfriend to please her mother in the first episode of the comedy series following the lives and loves of five young adults from Runcorn. With Sheridan Smith, Ralf Little, Beverley Callard, Will Mellor and Natalie Casey

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise starts losing weight and gets stretch marks, Jonny decides to stop being Mr Nice Guy, and Gaz gets cold feet over his relationship with Donna. Comedy, starring Ralf Little and Will Mellor

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their father's love of the outdoors

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: It's Tommy's birthday, but there's no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving he's still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Dane Buckley hosts the contest at Mandela Hall, Belfast, where five comics compete in the last of six knockout heats to bag their place in the grand final. Award-winning comedian and podcaster Alison Spittle and former NCA finalist William Thompson join head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri to assess the potential of contenders Eoghan Collins, Pravanya Pillay, Vinny Shiu, Lianna Holston and Jim Midge

programme.title: The Heart of Me (2003)
programme.desc: A bohemian artist in 1930s England falls for her handsome brother-in-law - and when he makes his illicit intentions clear, they begin a passionate affair, unaware the eventual discovery of their betrayal will lead to trauma and tragedy. Period drama based on Rosamond Lehmann's novel, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Paul Bettany, Olivia Williams and Eleanor Bron

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Exploring nuclear fusion - the holy grail for limitless clean energy - and whether it will come soon enough to avoid the worst of the climate crisis

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton see if Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Gladiator Apollo can sort fact from fiction. Today's adventure takes them to Bristol

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast city centre Adam pursues a fleeing suspect on foot. A few minutes later, he must do it again, as the man bursts out of the police car, trying to escape. Back at the station, Anna and Perri can't resist the temptation to hide the boss's car, temporarily. Meanwhile, the whole team leap into action when a stolen car fails to stop

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Lisa swap Northampton for a Powys life reboot with her children and takes to a canoe, 128 feet above the ground

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Joe Swift are in Wilmslow to help Vin and Reena transform their chaotic patch of grass, gravel and concrete into a zen and grow-your-own garden for family healing with a budget of £4,000

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty Don is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. Ashley Edwards visits Benton End in Suffolk, a place of artistic vision and horticultural legacy. And in Penarth, Sue Kent uncovers a rich mix of gardens, personalities and inspiration

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Live coverage of day four of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring singles matches from the second round. Four British players were involved in round two last year, but Emma Raducanu was the only one who progressed, overcoming Slovakian Rebecca ?ramková 6-4, 6-1, while Katie Boulter, Heather Watson and Sonay Kartal all lost out

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges ahead of Friday's final

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Sarajevo, where he traces the fateful last journey in 1914 of the Archduke Franz Ferdinand and his wife, Sophie, which ended in their assassination and led to the outbreak of the First World War. In the old quarter of Bosnia's capital he also admires the rich cultural heritage of the Ottoman Empire

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson casts his eye over the industrious village of Seahouses in Northumberland. He learns how the lethal shoreline shaped its transformation from a sleepy farming outpost to a thriving seaport with a bustling trade in eco-tourism, and uncovers how a family of Scottish entrepreneurs supercharged the herring industry that helped shape the village

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: From Fukushima to California, Hannah explores whether rewilding can help to heal the environmental wounds we have inflicted on our planet.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry takes a drive to the National Highways control centre for the UK's busiest motorway to meet the team that keep the motorways running 24/7

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the annual series charting the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How soccer in the USA would experience a second coming, with the likes of David Beckham heading to States, after the boom and bust of the 1980s. By the late 1970s, the NASL was continuing to attract the biggest footballing stars in the world, with new innovations making the sport attractive to its American audience. However, the good times didn't last, and the NASL was in a dire financial situation by the mid-1980s, seeing the end of the league, as the money and interest dried up. However, the college system helped fuel the rise of the US Women's National Team, whose World Cup and Olympic triumphs transformed women's football globally, while Beckham's arrival in MLS in 2007 reignited the men's game before Lionel Messi took Inter Miami and MLS to new heights in 2023

programme.title: Britney at the BBC
programme.desc: Her explosive arrival on the music scene aged just 16 made Britney Spears a frequent visitor to some of TV's biggest shows, and this programme captures the best of those performances, with hits including Baby One More Time, Crazy, Overprotected, Toxic and Oops! I Did It Again

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day with Adam Fleming

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Britney Spears was one of the biggest pop stars of her generation, and like many music stars, she was defined by imagery. But she was also the subject of extraordinary exploitation and control. Years of personal and professional turmoil would play out in the frenzied glare of the media spotlight. With the aid of 10 photos, this is the story of a manufactured child star fighting for control over her music, her life and, ultimately, her image

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final week continues with the Chef's Table, in which the amateurs serve food to six leading restaurateurs, including Daniel Clifford, Nieves Barragan and Paul Ainsworth. They are mentored by Aktar Islam of Opheem, a two-Michelin-starred Indian restaurant in Birmingham, and must each serve up a single dish to impress the professionals. As if that were not enough, the finalists then face a cook-off, putting their own twist on a classic dish. And for one hopeful, this will be the end of the road

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the semi-finalists head to the stadium of Premier League football club Brighton and Hove Albion to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Brighton fan Fatboy Slim and players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby pop in to see how they're getting on, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by designer Shayne Brady to assess the final results

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe feels liberated at a Gods and Monsters party hosted by eccentric local expats Flick and James, while Dan spirals into a dark place back at the villa. News of their separation destabilises each of the friends as the party takes unexpected turns. Drama, starring Jessica Raine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts sees the warning signs directly spelt out to him at a Merseyside property. Jacqui Joseph meets four friends in Suffolk embarking on their first property project. And Dion Dublin is in Yorkshire where he wants to fill a void

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Documentary following police officers in Bermuda. A vulnerable elderly man sparks an island-wide search for answers, a roadside stop uncovers a lethal switchblade, and airport officers track suspicious luggage.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From samurai armour to rare wildlife, this episode explores remarkable collections and the experts preserving them. At Snowshill Manor in the Cotswolds, Charles Paget Wade's 22,000 curiosities include rare Japanese armour, with a child's samurai suit restored for exhibition at the British Museum. And at Calke Abbey in Derbyshire, specialists protect historic taxidermy collections

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Live coverage of day five of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the singles quarter-finals. The last remaining British player was eliminated at this stage last year, when Emma Raducanu lost 6-2, 6-4 to top seed Zheng Qinwen, and Zheng's semi-final opponent was confirmed as Amanda Anisimova, after she overcame Emma Navarro 6-3, 6-3. In the other side of the draw, Tatjana Maria and Madison Keys prevailed against Elena Rybakina and Diana Shnaider respectively

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts make a final push for quizzing victory on Double Points Friday before Richard Osman hands over the trophy

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo enjoys a trip on the historic line from Sarajevo to Mostar, descending 600 metres through the Dinaric Alps, reflecting on the divisions which tore apart the old Yugoslavia. The walled city of Dubrovnik is Michael's final destination, where he enjoys its traditional music and dancing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Clara Amfo present the topical magazine-style programme that showcases stories of all varieties from all over the UK, ranging from the comic to the tragic, as well as interviews with guest stars

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson and Ruth Vichos are back in the Beechgrove Garden just outside Aberdeen, where they assess how the recent mixed climate has affected the crops and blooms. George is in the fruit cage checking for pests and pruning the Beechgrove fig, while Ruth is in her vertical plot, ideal for anyone with a small garden or little space to grow, where she will be planting a perennial that will give a crop year after year

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don brings a burst of colour to the jewel garden with vibrant annuals, plants out tender veg, and shares practical advice for tackling those greenhouse pests. Arit Anderson visits a striking garden in Greater Manchester packed with clever design solutions and wildlife-friendly planting that comes alive by day and night. In Dorset, Joe Swift explores a much-loved family garden reimagined for three generations. In Essex, a gardener has transformed a shaded, north-facing plot into a lush, lawn-free haven, while in Bristol, a Morris dancer channels movement and rhythm into her planting as she prepares for Gardeners' World Live in Birmingham

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From the conservation of an apprentice indenture from 1785 that tied a 13-year-old boy to a cotton mill, to the unusual homes of working families at Kinver Edge carved into a sandstone hillside, this episode uncovers the hidden lives of ordinary working people. It also follows the teams making sure their stories are never forgotten. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith's darkly comic anthology returns. Pantalone is planning the heist of the century - £12 million in rough-cut diamonds. But if you're going to hire a bunch of complete clowns to carry out a robbery, and half of them are planning to double-cross the other half, you've not just got a recipe for disaster, you've actually got the ingredients of a Tarantino Commedia dell'Arte. Starring Kevin Bishop, Gemma Whelan and Paterson Joseph

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Queer (2024)
programme.desc: When William Lee meets an enigmatic student in Mexico City, he becomes obsessed and begins a desperate journey into darkness. Romantic drama, starring Daniel Craig and Jason Schwartzman

programme.title: Queen & Slim (2019)
programme.desc: A black couple's first date takes a shocking turn when a traffic stop leads to the killing of the police officer in self-defence, and the fugitive pair hit the road for a life-changing journey. When a video of the incident goes viral, the unwitting outlaws soon become a symbol of trauma, terror, grief and pain for people all across the United States. Crime drama, starring Daniel Kaluuya, Jodie Turner-Smith and Bokeem Woodbine

programme.title: Panorama

programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: In their final challenge, the amateur cooks serve a three-course menu that delivers in presentation, taste and originality, hoping they will have done enough to impress Anna Haugh and Grace Dent and be crowned MasterChef Champion 2026. This will no doubt be a tight race as the finalists draw out every ounce of culinary know-how in the hope of coming out on top

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-presenter Sarah Cruddas head to central Scotland, which has its own 'Area 51', known as the Falkirk Triangle, encompassing the now infamous village of Bonnybridge, which claims the highest concentration of the region's UFO activity. They also investigate the alleged abduction of forestry worker Robert Taylor in 1979, the only British UFO incident to have been officially investigated by the police

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Mutant Mermaid, UFOs vs Nukes and Seamonster Chases a Boat
programme.desc: Scientists study claims of a dead mermaid discovered and preserved in Japan. Plus a glowing-eyed sea creature captured on video chasing a motorboat and the connection between a man who lived alone in the jungle for over a quarter of a century and a fabled lost city in the Amazon

programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: An original Beach Boys surfboard, a magazine feature penned by Mark Twain, an early spirit hydrometer and an original Peter Max painting of the Statue of Liberty are assessed

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and the gang encounter seeds that were sent into space, a spaceship toilet seat and a photo autographed by famous astronauts

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur EJ Osborne heads to Working Recycling Centre seeking to rescue and revamp three pieces of tip-bound trash. programme.desc: Staff fight over whether they should buy a batch of venomous reptiles, and an incident forces Les to seek a new head of security

programme.desc: Les tells a frightening story of a hold-up at his grandfather's store, and a dominatrix brings her gear into the shop

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ancient alien therorists investigate evidence suggesting that extraterrestrials have played a pivotal role in the progress of mankind

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, one of the most spectacular mass UFO sighting of all time took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Despite hours of video and photographic evidence this case remains unexplained. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness nurse David Parker who provides a vivid description of his close-up encounter with a huge boomerang-shaped UFO that passed directly overhead. They also investigate the response by the authorities in the days following the UFO event

programme.desc: The Thornhill buyers run for cover when a self-help CD gives Roy a new lease on his egomaniacal life

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts head to Aylesbury in Buckinghamshire. Corrie Jeffery wants to snap up a Leica camera, James Layte takes a liking to a flowery brooch, Simon Schneider is charmed by a bracelet and Henry Nicholls uncovers various valuable items

programme.desc: Eye-witness accounts and footage, plus expert analysis and innovative special effects show what UFOs might see from their own perspective

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The dark history of the Cecil Hotel in downtown Los Angeles, with stories of murders, suicides and serial killers. Little wonder there are always vacancies there

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Delving into the mysteries surrounding the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, investigating the theories that he remained alive, secretly, for some time afterwards

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: Family members are terrorised by a supernatural beast near their trailer park, and a woman adopts a dog only to find it's not the only thing she's taken from the pound

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, one of the most spectacular mass UFO sighting of all time took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Despite hours of video and photographic evidence this case remains unexplained. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness nurse David Parker who provides a vivid description of his close-up encounter with a huge boomerang-shaped UFO that passed directly overhead. They also investigate the response by the authorities in the days following the UFO event

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Sabotage, Ghost Child and Hungry Hungry Sasquatch
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film'. A species of carnivorous marsupials, thought to be long extinct, are suspected of being caught on camera. Plus, the notion that a visitor at a historic cemetery in Savannah captured a 'ghost child'

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley buys a 1950s Eljer toilet for $10, leading to Seth to purchase it for double the price in order that he can destroy it with a sledgehammer and storm out of the store

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth chooses to stay at home and calm down after his altercation with Ashley. However, the computer system goes down, so he has no choice but to come in and repair the system

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth is excited when a clergyman sells him a rare baseball signed by Babe Ruth, complete with authentication papers - and knows it could be worth several thousand dollars

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts head to Aylesbury in Buckinghamshire. Corrie Jeffery wants to snap up a Leica camera, James Layte takes a liking to a flowery brooch, Simon Schneider is charmed by a bracelet and Henry Nicholls uncovers various valuable items

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A seller brings in an unusual diamond crafted from the DNA of a musical genius. A vintage book collection also comes in, and Chum has a birthday surprise for Rick

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.desc: An auction near some of the gang's old high school proves they are all still rowdy teenagers at heart - but it remains to be seen who will have to stay behind for detention

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: An original Beach Boys surfboard, a magazine feature penned by Mark Twain, an early spirit hydrometer and an original Peter Max painting of the Statue of Liberty are assessed

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and the gang encounter seeds that were sent into space, a spaceship toilet seat and a photo autographed by famous astronauts

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur EJ Osborne heads to Working Recycling Centre seeking to rescue and revamp three pieces of tip-bound trash. An old gas meter box is worked on by robot-maker Mark Haig and a tired table and chairs are given a creative once over by carpenter Bruce Faulseit. EJ has some racing tyres to rev his creative engine, but will all three transformations succeed in making a profit?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ten discoveries and breakthroughs that challenge traditional beliefs about human history. They include a lost city hidden in the Andes, mysterious metal spheres that are not of this world and a sunken city that is said to be the home of the gods

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-host Sarah Cruddas examine the Chicago O'Hare International Airport case of November 2006 - the sighting of a flying saucer hovering over a United Airlines airport gate. The incident was witnessed by multiple aviation professionals, pilots and passengers. The immediate aftermath of the event was captured in real time by audio recordings of communication between the airport's Air Traffic Control tower and airline staff, but the sighting was dismissed by the US government's Federal Aviation Administration as a weather phenomenon. Craig and Sarah talk to UFO investigator Robert Powell, who reveals the UFO punched a circular hole in the clouds as it departed upwards after hovering over the terminal. Craig and Sarah also learn astonishing details about how aviation authorities handled the incident

programme.desc: Paul's penchant for lockers reaches new heights at a Wasaga Beach auction

programme.desc: Real-life incidents, featuring from a thief smashing into glass doors to a toddler being swept onto an airport luggage belt,

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, including the likes of Vernon Kay and Paddy McGuinness, Denise Van Outen and Johnny Vaughan, Vicky Pattison and Pete Wicks, Bez and Shaun Ryder, Ashley Banjo and Perri Kiely, Rylan and mum Linda, and Roman and Martin Kemp sit down to give their sharp, insightful and funny thoughts on the week's TV

programme.title: Building Monaco's Superhomes
programme.desc: Property developer Guy Phoenix buys and renovates a £16million apartment in Monaco, the biggest investment and financial risk he has ever taken. In this second of two programmes, as he navigates the intricacies of building in one of the most expensive places to live on the planet, Guy comes under pressure to complete his luxury apartment in time for the upcoming Monaco Grand Prix. As the finish line gets closer, the pressure and stress on both him and his crack team of builders becomes almost unbearable

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by Alan Davies to sample an action-packed weekend in Trieste, northern Italy. Trieste is famous for the Bora, the north-easterly wind said to shape the city and its inhabitants. Joe and Alan discover this at the Wind Museum, where curator Rino presides over a collection of wind samples donated from all over the world. A few miles north the pair enjoy a food and fizz pairing in the village of Prosecco. Fortunately, they're in the right place for hangovers as Trieste is Italy's caffeine capital. No mini break is complete without learning a new skill to brag about when you get home, so Joe and Alan attend a painting class

programme.title: Rosaline (2022)
programme.desc: A member of an aristocratic family is dumped by her suitor Romeo in favour of her own cousin Juliet. Outraged at the betrayal, she sets out to sabotage the most famous love story in history. Romantic comedy, starring Kaitlyn Dever and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the £1,000 prize in Swindon, where Carla and Hayley kick off the competition by serving up a spicy Caribbean-themed menu for food snob Jeanette and her husband Rob, and lovebirds Tim and Kath. However, spice-loving Carla causes upset by revealing she plans on bringing her own hot pepper sauce to the other couples' dinner parties

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family enhance their new greenhouse to make it well and truly part of their home, as it turned into a sanctuary for Sarah to escape to during her chemotherapy treatment. Sarah and Graham want to create warmth for people as well as plants, so they transform the greenhouse into a welcoming outdoor room complete with an antique wood burner. Alongside the greenhouse transformation, the family also put on a chicken rehoming event

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla's second wedding anniversary arrives and she's convinced that Eddie will remember to send her a gift, even though he's travelling with the ice show. Meanwhile, Woody is gripped by gambling fever

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam grows tired of wooing Rebecca and decides to switch his amorous attentions to her sister instead - but ends up with a dead body on his hands. Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Celebrated marriage guidance expert Dr Lawrence Crandell, an old friend of Frasier's, visits town on a book tour, but in between preaching marital fidelity he makes a pass at Rebecca. Kirstie Alley and Ted Danson star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy chats non-stop through a basketball game, and Ray responds by suggesting to Robert that she talks too much - a criticism she does not take well. Comedy, starring Monica Horan and Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra cannot understand why Ray keeps turning down her advances - not realising that playing it cool is all part of his latest devious plan to get what he wants. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Pat asks Robert to keep her cigarette habit a secret - but he later has to come up with an excuse when Amy smells smoke on him, leading to a string of revelations for the family

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier are excited to learn they may be descended from Russian royalty, and Martin appears on the Antiques Roadshow. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles tries to settle a score with a plastic surgeon, only to find himself attracted to her, while Frasier is disappointed by the lack of achievements listed in his obituary when it is published prematurely

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick does his best to get Frasier and Lilith back together during a Thanksgiving visit. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, Bebe Neuwirth and Trevor Einhorn

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. A bout of crippling back pain prompts Frasier to begin taking strong medication - which dulls his senses and causes him to accidentally blurt out a long-held secret to Daphne. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Expert buyers Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz hunt down hidden treasures and valuable relics across the USA, uncovering incredible artefacts and captivating stories of American life and history. The guys go big in Texas with a mega-pick at the first Dr Pepper bottling plant and tough negotiations in a 50,000 square-foot warehouse

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend come to the rescue of their first-ever custom car - a radically modified VW Beetle called a VW Wizard

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: A family feel the financial strain of trying to resurrect an Italian ghost village. The owners of a crumbling French hamlet attempt to save the decaying village bakery and its roof, while those in charge of a restored village in the Italian hills prepare to hold their biggest event yet

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Michael and his sister Catherine live many miles apart but they're planning on seeing a lot more of each other as they pool their resources to buy a holiday home together. They've chosen Manilva on the Costa del Sol and need somewhere with enough space to share with their immediate families and wider family circle. For their budget of £175,000, Jean Johansson lines up five exciting options. Can she find their perfect holiday home in the sun?

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: On the Ashby Canal, floating candlemaker Connor gears up for the first day of the trading season as he heads to Whitchurch, and crosses paths with floating favourite The Curry Cruiser. Peter and Noni continue their breathtaking Scottish escapade, while Tom discovers that mooring a narrowboat can be anything but straightforward. And the Canal River Trust team tackle a stubborn stretch of weeds threatening to clog up the cut near Wolverhampton

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: At Château Destinée, the pressure's on for Linda and Peter to restore riverside steps for a wedding. Matt and Amelia search for their forever family chateau. And in Normandy, Steve and Tim transform a tatty bathroom into an Italian-inspired breakfast room

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, mother and son Sharon and Shaun Garner help restaurateurs Alison and Steve relocate from the Costa del Sol. With a maximum budget of 130,000 euros the heat is on to find the perfect place for the couple and their precious pooches. Meanwhile, Shaun asks husband Salva and niece and nephew Luca and Alicia to help surprise Steve and Sharon on their ruby wedding anniversary. In Alicante, sisters Sophie and Charlotte are with Ken and Christine from Newcastle-upon-Tyne, who want a holiday home to enjoy in their imminent retirement. With a budget of 100,000 euros, outdoor space for their grandkids is their top priority

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Worsley in Greater Manchester, George helps Katherine and Andrew to renovate their new home's awkward extension and split-level living space, and reveal the home's stunning view, while landscaper Luke tackles the steeply sloped, hazardous rear garden

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and at the end of this episode, one of the five competitors will walk away with a trophy that looks very little like Greg Davies tucked under their arm. But before that, Amy Gledhill literally tears her hair out, Armando Iannucci plays the recorder while riding a trike with a plunger on his head, Joel Dommett sniffs the contents of a vacuum, Kumail Nanjiani mistreats a beloved resident of the Taskmaster house and Joanna Page lays waste to Little Alex Horne. Plus, the greatest pillow fights of all time

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: Loyal chauffeur Kabir wants the team to tell the life story of his boss, hard-working financier Mr Eblen Archer. Sam thinks it seems like such a heart-warming idea, until he realises there isn't much of a story to tell. However, Pat knows the rich and famous always have sordid secrets, so the team go in search of scandals their subject is involved in

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: A couple become trapped in an underground cave, so Sam and his crew respond immediately to the national emergency by planning a movie that they can send down to lift their spirits. However, to complicate matters, an actual rescue team are also on site, who keep getting in the movie-makers' way - and who might just save the couple before the movie is finished. Offbeat comedy, written by and starring Sam Campbell

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Outsider
programme.desc: Footage from the comedian's 2016 stage show, recorded at the Brighton Dome. The plain-speaking Geordie stand-up shares her thoughts on moving to the countryside, getting a rescue dog and her struggles with irritable bowel syndrome

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: When Clive meets Diane at work, the chance of a romance leads him to confess secrets that he's kept quiet for years. But when Clive and Leo discover the truth about Calico Road, they're thrown into open warfare. Marie, Saul and George have no choice but to join the battle, as the two households conspire against each other. But Clive's fury won't be stopped, and the danger to Leo gets closer

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Leo and Clive stand alone, face-to-face, in the ultimate confrontation on a night of terror. Meanwhile, Zee reaches out to help George, and they embark on a joyous voyage of discovery. It's a wild night on the streets of Manchester, as violence explodes, souls are laid bare, dangerous games are played and decisions are made that will have terrible consequences for everyone

programme.title: Little Richard: I Am Everything (2023)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the life of the one-of-a-kind rock 'n' roll icon who shaped the world of music. Contributors include John Waters, Mick Jagger and Tom Jones

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Wiltshire, civil partners Andrew and David serve up a 'ritzy' affair for their guests, but their food gets a mixed reaction. On the second night, hosts Emma and Richard face a grilling over their use of shop-bought ingredients. Meanwhile, Richard and Joey's 'bromance' develops when they feed each other heart-shaped strawberries. Last to host are newlyweds Joey and Hannah, who serve basic wholesome food, but argue over the size of their fishcakes

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family prepare the farm for the arrival of two piglets. After building an enclosure, the piglets arrive and get settled into their new home - but it isn't long before they've escaped, leaving Graham the task of locating the playful porkers. Keen to immerse themselves in all the West Country has to offer, the family also head to Stonehenge for the autumn equinox sunrise

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rebecca fears for her sanity after having erotic dreams about Sam just after an old flame has walked back into her life. Kirstie Alley and Ted Danson star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. When multi-millionaire Robin Colcord tells Rebecca she is the sole reason he visits Cheers, she forgets her intention to give Sam a chance and takes off for a weekend with him instead

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam seeks a new challenge and decides to run a bar on his own to take his mind off Rebecca's relationship with millionaire Robin Colcord. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: In the last-ever episode of the American comedy, Ray has a brush with death while undergoing minor surgery, but pulls through - leaving his family uncertain over whether to tell him what happened

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra tires of having to deal with Ray's interfering mother, grumpy father and resentful brother, who constantly show up out of the blue, and a white lie has embarrassing consequences. Pilot episode of the comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra sees newlywed friends making a public display of affection, and cannot help wondering why Raymond has so much trouble telling her he loves her. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles clashes with girlfriend Mel over a misunderstanding, Daphne picks up the wrong signals, and friends are thin on the ground at Frasier's Christmas party. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip in the Winnebago on New Year's Eve to celebrate the turn of the new millennium, but a case of mistaken identity leaves Frasier and Martin trying to rescue Niles from another family's van

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink pulls out all the stops to compose a new theme tune for his radio show - but fails to appreciate what is required. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is delighted to land a date with a former prom queen, but soon learns the hard way that beauty is only skin deep. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The team deal with a California who is under pressure to downsize, and later play a hunch that turns up a lot of rare vintage cars

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Southampton to take on a 1969 Jensen Interceptor. Owner Nick cherished his dream car until he got extreme arthritis

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: Building work at a Portuguese village finally gathers pace, there are wedding bells at a rebuilt Italian borgo, and the owners of an ancient French hamlet reach a major milestone as they open their glamping site to paying guests

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King heads to Spain's Mar Menor to help Eileen from Birmingham find the perfect holiday home, where she can take time out from her demanding job. To also assist her with the search, Eileen's brings along her daughter Khaya, who plans to visit regularly. Eileen ideally wants a two-bedroom apartment near the beach, and has a maximum budget of £43,500

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Peter and Noni's Scottish adventure draws to a majestic close in Edinburgh, and Connor is all aglow selling candles at the Whitchurch Boat Festival on the Llangollen. Tom takes a big step in his new floating life, cooking lunch for his parents aboard for the very first time. The Canal and River Trust continue their mission to keep the UK's waterways shipshape and weed-free

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: Deanne and Richard dig deep to create a new pool in Bordeaux. Matt and Amelia edge closer to their own slice of chateau life. And after months of hard graft, Janet and Philip unveil their stunning stairwell transformation

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Steve Garner and his son Shaun are on the hunt for a Spanish property for Lara from Leicestershire, and her mum Jennie, who currently lives in Majorca. However, Lara prefers modern styles while Jennie favours traditional. In Alicante, Sophie and her husband Damon are searching with Adrian and Debbie from Plymouth, who have a top budget of 130,000 euros for the Spanish escape of their dreams

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With the cost of living leaving many people struggling to stay afloat, Dispatches meets the everyday people pushed into exploitative and dangerous situations. Last year, almost two million people in Britain turned to illegal money lenders for help. And some are resorting to taking out unregulated loans offered on social media. Reporter Ellie Flynn goes undercover to meet the men exploiting cash-strapped and vulnerable people, uncovering sexual predators who prey on women who are struggling to make ends meet and organised criminals recruiting unknowing victims. She also hears from the people forced into tough choices to pay the bills - from a nurse who has become a 'full-service escort' to a police officer who is postponing thoughts of retirement to work two jobs

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, including the likes of Vernon Kay and Paddy McGuinness, Denise Van Outen and Johnny Vaughan, Vicky Pattison and Pete Wicks, Bez and Shaun Ryder, Ashley Banjo and Perri Kiely, Rylan and mum Linda, and Roman and Martin Kemp sit down to give their sharp, insightful and funny thoughts on the week's TV

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Forty-six year-old ex-Traitors contestant Ash prepares for a date with 46-year-old self-confessed cheesy Spanish poet Miguel. However, feeling less than adept at flirting, she turns to Fred Sirieix for a crash-course in romantic banter. Luxury sales assistant Dominic is looking for a geek with heart who can handle his big personality. Enter Stephen, a sound engineer armed with a library of flirty one-liners. Self-proclaimed smoothie Wayne from Kent is a handyman who can fix almost anything - except his love life. With a ukulele in hand and chat-up lines at the ready, he prepares to serenade music-lover Teresa

programme.title: Jojo Rabbit (2019)
programme.desc: A lonely German boy discovers that his single mother is hiding a Jewish girl in their attic. Aided only by his imaginary friend, Adolf Hitler, Jojo Rabbit must confront his blind nationalism as the Second World War continues to rage on. Taika Waititi wrote, directed and co-stars in this Oscar-winning comedy, featuring Roman Griffin Davis, Thomasin McKenzie, Rebel Wilson, Stephen Merchant and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and at the end of this episode, one of the five competitors will walk away with a trophy that looks very little like Greg Davies tucked under their arm. But before that, Amy Gledhill literally tears her hair out, Armando Iannucci plays the recorder while riding a trike with a plunger on his head, Joel Dommett sniffs the contents of a vacuum, Kumail Nanjiani mistreats a beloved resident of the Taskmaster house and Joanna Page lays waste to Little Alex Horne. Plus, the greatest pillow fights of all time

programme.title: The Hate U Give (2018)
programme.desc: Starr Carter lives in two worlds - the poor, black neighbourhood where she resides and the mostly white prep school she attends. When she witnesses the fatal shooting of her childhood friend by a policeman, Starr must find her voice and stand up for what's right. Drama, starring Amandla Stenberg, Regina Hall and Russell Hornsby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: When Clive meets Diane at work, the chance of a romance leads him to confess secrets that he's kept quiet for years. But when Clive and Leo discover the truth about Calico Road, they're thrown into open warfare. Marie, Saul and George have no choice but to join the battle, as the two households conspire against each other. But Clive's fury won't be stopped, and the danger to Leo gets closer

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by Alan Davies to sample an action-packed weekend in Trieste, northern Italy. Trieste is famous for the Bora, the north-easterly wind said to shape the city and its inhabitants. Joe and Alan discover this at the Wind Museum, where curator Rino presides over a collection of wind samples donated from all over the world. A few miles north the pair enjoy a food and fizz pairing in the village of Prosecco. Fortunately, they're in the right place for hangovers as Trieste is Italy's caffeine capital. No mini break is complete without learning a new skill to brag about when you get home, so Joe and Alan attend a painting class

programme.title: Rosaline (2022)
programme.desc: A member of an aristocratic family is dumped by her suitor Romeo in favour of her own cousin Juliet. Outraged at the betrayal, she sets out to sabotage the most famous love story in history. Romantic comedy, starring Kaitlyn Dever and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the £1,000 prize in Swindon, where Carla and Hayley kick off the competition by serving up a spicy Caribbean-themed menu for food snob Jeanette and her husband Rob, and lovebirds Tim and Kath. However, spice-loving Carla causes upset by revealing she plans on bringing her own hot pepper sauce to the other couples' dinner parties

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family enhance their new greenhouse to make it well and truly part of their home, as it turned into a sanctuary for Sarah to escape to during her chemotherapy treatment. Sarah and Graham want to create warmth for people as well as plants, so they transform the greenhouse into a welcoming outdoor room complete with an antique wood burner. Alongside the greenhouse transformation, the family also put on a chicken rehoming event

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla's second wedding anniversary arrives and she's convinced that Eddie will remember to send her a gift, even though he's travelling with the ice show. Meanwhile, Woody is gripped by gambling fever

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam grows tired of wooing Rebecca and decides to switch his amorous attentions to her sister instead - but ends up with a dead body on his hands. Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Celebrated marriage guidance expert Dr Lawrence Crandell, an old friend of Frasier's, visits town on a book tour, but in between preaching marital fidelity he makes a pass at Rebecca. Kirstie Alley and Ted Danson star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy chats non-stop through a basketball game, and Ray responds by suggesting to Robert that she talks too much - a criticism she does not take well. Comedy, starring Monica Horan and Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra cannot understand why Ray keeps turning down her advances - not realising that playing it cool is all part of his latest devious plan to get what he wants. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Pat asks Robert to keep her cigarette habit a secret - but he later has to come up with an excuse when Amy smells smoke on him, leading to a string of revelations for the family

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier are excited to learn they may be descended from Russian royalty, and Martin appears on the Antiques Roadshow. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles tries to settle a score with a plastic surgeon, only to find himself attracted to her, while Frasier is disappointed by the lack of achievements listed in his obituary when it is published prematurely

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick does his best to get Frasier and Lilith back together during a Thanksgiving visit. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, Bebe Neuwirth and Trevor Einhorn

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. A bout of crippling back pain prompts Frasier to begin taking strong medication - which dulls his senses and causes him to accidentally blurt out a long-held secret to Daphne. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: Deanne and Richard dig deep to create a new pool in Bordeaux. Matt and Amelia edge closer to their own slice of chateau life. And after months of hard graft, Janet and Philip unveil their stunning stairwell transformation

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Steve Garner and his son Shaun are on the hunt for a Spanish property for Lara from Leicestershire, and her mum Jennie, who currently lives in Majorca. However, Lara prefers modern styles while Jennie favours traditional. In Alicante, Sophie and her husband Damon are searching with Adrian and Debbie from Plymouth, who have a top budget of 130,000 euros for the Spanish escape of their dreams

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With the cost of living leaving many people struggling to stay afloat, Dispatches meets the everyday people pushed into exploitative and dangerous situations. Last year, almost two million people in Britain turned to illegal money lenders for help. And some are resorting to taking out unregulated loans offered on social media. Reporter Ellie Flynn goes undercover to meet the men exploiting cash-strapped and vulnerable people, uncovering sexual predators who prey on women who are struggling to make ends meet and organised criminals recruiting unknowing victims. She also hears from the people forced into tough choices to pay the bills - from a nurse who has become a 'full-service escort' to a police officer who is postponing thoughts of retirement to work two jobs

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, including the likes of Vernon Kay and Paddy McGuinness, Denise Van Outen and Johnny Vaughan, Vicky Pattison and Pete Wicks, Bez and Shaun Ryder, Ashley Banjo and Perri Kiely, Rylan and mum Linda, and Roman and Martin Kemp sit down to give their sharp, insightful and funny thoughts on the week's TV

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Forty-six year-old ex-Traitors contestant Ash prepares for a date with 46-year-old self-confessed cheesy Spanish poet Miguel. However, feeling less than adept at flirting, she turns to Fred Sirieix for a crash-course in romantic banter. Luxury sales assistant Dominic is looking for a geek with heart who can handle his big personality. Enter Stephen, a sound engineer armed with a library of flirty one-liners. Self-proclaimed smoothie Wayne from Kent is a handyman who can fix almost anything - except his love life. With a ukulele in hand and chat-up lines at the ready, he prepares to serenade music-lover Teresa

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Outsider
programme.desc: Footage from the comedian's 2016 stage show, recorded at the Brighton Dome. The plain-speaking Geordie stand-up shares her thoughts on moving to the countryside, getting a rescue dog and her struggles with irritable bowel syndrome

programme.title: The Hate U Give (2018)
programme.desc: Starr Carter lives in two worlds - the poor, black neighbourhood where she resides and the mostly white prep school she attends. When she witnesses the fatal shooting of her childhood friend by a policeman, Starr must find her voice and stand up for what's right. Drama, starring Amandla Stenberg, Regina Hall and Russell Hornsby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet

programme.title: Jimmy Carr's Am I the A**hole?

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Dirty Girl
programme.desc: Chandler gets on more intimate terms with Joey's girlfriend, and Ross's love life improves - until his latest conquest reveals a grubby secret. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer, Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Q and Sal call out odd names while working at an office. Later, the guys ask park-goers for help with online dating profiles

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
