programme.desc: AJ Odudu hosts as people bid for the work of an art forger, a Leeds football programme from 1904, a taxidermy unicorn and a set of drums from the 1970s

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: As the coach winds its way to Grandola in Portugal, poor time-keeping sets Brendan on the warpath, before two more newbies hop aboard

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh comes to the aid of the Costellos in Norwich, who have recently moved into a bungalow with a garden in desperate need of a makeover. In 2009, dad Ian was injured by a roadside bomb while serving in Afghanistan, and has since been rehoused with wife Lyndsey and their three children to a property offered specifically to ex-service people and their families. The team aims to provide areas of relaxation, beauty and play, with David Domoney finding a way to stimulate the kids' imagination on a budget

programme.title: Our Dream Farm with Matt Baker
programme.desc: Matt Baker helps the National Trust find a new tenant for the vacant Wallington Estate farm in Northumberland and its 340 acres of grassland, woodland and wetland. Matt follows the process as seven applicants for the 10-year lease spend three weeks demonstrating they have what it takes to be chosen for the position. In this first episode, Matt arrives at the estate to meet the seven applicants. It's lambing season, so the hopefuls get straight to work

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: I'm Not Feeling Myself Tonight (1976)
programme.desc: Hapless Jon Pigeon works in a sex research institute. To seduce a colleague, he invents an aphrodisiac-emitter machine turning any man or woman into a horny fiend. Comedy starring Barry Andrews, James Booth, Sally Faulkner.

programme.title: Battle of Britain: Empty Skies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team enter the high-stakes world of poker when Bobby Rodrigues, the favourite to win the Caribbean Masters tournament, is poisoned mid-game. As all fellow players are confined to their seats for the entire match, and no other individual is allowed access to the stage during that time, suspicion soon falls upon each player. A novice to poker, Jack enlists Dwayne's assistance in getting the players to show their hand, while Florence, who is forced to keep away from work after an injury, finds her own way to help the case. Nigel Planer, Mark Benton and Zoe Telford guest star

programme.desc: The detective's estranged father is suspected of murder after being found standing drunk next to a dead body. programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A murder plot emerges when a skeletal corpse falls through the ceiling and on to the stage during a performance of Macbeth. The body is identified as the former owner of the theatre who had died a year earlier, leading to the re-opening of his coffin. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. programme.desc: A celebrated Toronto industrialist and philanthropist dies after being struck on the back of the head outside his home, and as Murdoch and Dr Ogden examine the forensic evidence, they conclude a young person may be responsible. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson and Helene Joy

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A hospital is terrorised by a hooded and robed figure with a crossbow, who is exacting revenge on medics he apparently believes responsible of malpractice. A medium tells Murdoch she has seen the murders in a vision, but is unable to identify the killer. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Share fame

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.sub-title: A Stab in the Dark Web
programme.desc: As Major Crimes investigates a dark web marketplace, Charlie and Rex protect a witness in their home

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective's estranged father is suspected of murder after being found standing drunk next to a dead body. Murdoch questions his own impartiality after new evidence is found, while also experiencing flashbacks concerning the death of his mother after a family bust-up. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A murder plot emerges when a skeletal corpse falls through the ceiling and on to the stage during a performance of Macbeth. The body is identified as the former owner of the theatre who had died a year earlier, leading to the re-opening of his coffin. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.desc: It seems money doesn't buy happiness when lottery winner Cherry is found dead at her luxurious villa, much to the horror of her husband and their visiting friends. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Would I Lie to You? at Christmas
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Joe Lycett, Joe Swash and Ruth Madeley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for this festive edition of the comedy game show. Did one of the panellists really program a satnav so their mother's trip to Lakeside ended up in the Lake District? And would one of the captains really hold up a train to get his daughter's cuddly toy back? Or were they just festive fibs? Rob Brydon hosts the fun

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with another episode of memorable moments from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Stephen Fry presents an extended version of the quiz, and unleashes a batch of fiendishly puzzling questions to test the general knowledge - or ignorance - of Jo Brand, Liza Tarbuck, Sue Perkins and regular panellist Alan Davies, who reveal what they know about topics including jelly, jam and juice

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian examines the worth of online petitions, and reveals how he once beat Alex Reid - but not in a cage fight

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers discover bundles of cocaine hidden inside the wheel wells of an SUV, and two retirees are caught trying to smuggle weight loss medication across the border

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers discover a fuel tanker with bundles of cocaine hidden in the cab, and three men with fake IDs and a gruesome video of a murder on their phones are questioned

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the gang are back with another high-octane instalment of the popular motoring series. The Porsche 911 is put through its paces on the racetrack and the British Jaguar S type is pitted against its rivals, the American Chrysler 300C and the Australian Vauxhall Monaro VXR. Plus, a chance to help preserve an element of motoring history in Restoration. Comedian Bill Bailey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The Ford Focus is put through its paces, the Ferrari Enzo burns rubber on the track, and the supercars of the 1980s are pitted against their modern-day counterparts. There's a race between a child on a skateboard and a turbo-charged rally car, and Geri Horner is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Thailand by travelling to the beaches of the south and the mountains of the north to discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade created a rich and varied cuisine. They meet a coconut-picking monkey, head out to sea during a monsoon for a nerve-racking fishing expedition and cook a curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall

programme.desc: Professor Hannah Fry hosts an extended edition of the current affairs quiz, with comedians Zoe Lyons and Ed Patrick joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop

programme.desc: Team captain Aisling Bea is joined by Joel Dommett and James Acaster, while on the opposing side, Rob Beckett teams up with Joe Swash and Roisin Conaty to discuss memorable moments from school as well as topics that set the great British public's chins wagging. Jimmy Carr hosts

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis front a special edition of the show featuring unseen material, outtakes, and all the usual fun

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef champions the food, history and culture of the county, foraging for mussels in his favourite rock pools, and touring Bodmin Moor's dark past with his son Jack. He also cooks a fool-proof souffle with artisan Cornish goat's cheese

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick makes heritage cider with Raynor Winn, author of The Salt Path, and cooks chicken, leek and cider gratin for his friend John Harris, the head gardener at Tresillian House, who regales Rick with his memories of cider-making as a boy. The chef also meets a sea shanty group with a difference

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Land Rover Discovery 3 through its paces when he takes it off road. The BMW 1 Series' progress in the small hatchback market is investigated, the world's first hydrofoil car is revealed and Joanna Lumley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The Aston Martin Vanquish S is pitted against the Ferrari 575, while James May and Richard Hammond spend 24 hours in the Smart Forfour. Comedian Jimmy Carr features as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by fulfilling a lifetime's ambition to visit Japan, the home of their favourite food. The duo sample sushi at Tokyo's famous fish market and have the privilege of spending time in a sumo stable, where they take part in a wrestling bout

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef champions the food, history and culture of the county, foraging for mussels in his favourite rock pools, and touring Bodmin Moor's dark past with his son Jack. He also cooks a fool-proof souffle with artisan Cornish goat's cheese

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick makes heritage cider with Raynor Winn, author of The Salt Path, and cooks chicken, leek and cider gratin for his friend John Harris, the head gardener at Tresillian House, who regales Rick with his memories of cider-making as a boy. The chef also meets a sea shanty group with a difference

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: It's been over a year since Frank's death and Wendy, rediscovering a long lost love for painting, decides to create a very unique tribute to him in oils. The usually curmudgeonly Geoffrey seems very keen on the commemorative artwork, but Lee and Lucy simply hate it. Once it's hanging on their wall, the pair contrive to 'lose' the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Deborah Grant and Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the silly mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she read it. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers discover bundles of cocaine hidden inside the wheel wells of an SUV, and two retirees are caught trying to smuggle weight loss medication across the border

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers discover a fuel tanker with bundles of cocaine hidden in the cab, and three men with fake IDs and a gruesome video of a murder on their phones are questioned

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the gang are back with another high-octane instalment of the popular motoring series. The Porsche 911 is put through its paces on the racetrack and the British Jaguar S type is pitted against its rivals, the American Chrysler 300C and the Australian Vauxhall Monaro VXR. Plus, a chance to help preserve an element of motoring history in Restoration. Comedian Bill Bailey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The Ford Focus is put through its paces, the Ferrari Enzo burns rubber on the track, and the supercars of the 1980s are pitted against their modern-day counterparts. There's a race between a child on a skateboard and a turbo-charged rally car, and Geri Horner is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Thailand by travelling to the beaches of the south and the mountains of the north to discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade created a rich and varied cuisine. They meet a coconut-picking monkey, head out to sea during a monsoon for a nerve-racking fishing expedition and cook a curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The Aston Martin Vanquish S is pitted against the Ferrari 575, while James May and Richard Hammond spend 24 hours in the Smart Forfour. Comedian Jimmy Carr features as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by fulfilling a lifetime's ambition to visit Japan, the home of their favourite food. programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A family Christmas party turns sour when Lucy announces that she has got tickets to see Jason Donovan in Panto. Lee loathes Panto - especially given the Aussie actor was Lucy's teenage crush. That night, Lee has an unsettling dream in which he is dressed as Buttons and his whole life has turned into a Pantomime. Lucy is Cinderella, Toby and Anna are the Ugly Sisters, Geoffrey is Baron Hardup and Wendy is Fairy Godmother. Worse still, Prince Charming - the handsome suitor who pursues Cinderella and asks for her hand in marriage - is Jason Donovan. Can Buttons keep the fairy-tale couple apart? Festive edition of the comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, and featuring a guest appearance by a certain former soap star

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Michelle grows suspicious when she receives two enquiries about the forthcoming Alzheimer's drug trial in one day and does some investigating. Vivien comes to work to try to take her mind off waiting for the jury to reach a verdict, but does not know how to move on with her life once the case is closed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric goes on the warpath after Connie's plans to start a cardiology centre at the hospital meet with disaster when a patient of his nearly dies, but she is reluctant to abandon her dream so easily. Owen finds it increasingly difficult to communicate with his daughter, especially after she tells Diane their marriage won't last

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Linda is forced to take her errant sister's children to the ED so she can supervise them. However, the youngsters cause chaos, and matters come to a head when one of them steals a baby to get back at her aunt. Meanwhile, Dylan struggles to treat a homeless alcoholic who was involved in a traffic accident

programme.desc: An overjoyed Alfie welcomes Michael into his home, but why is an unhappy Kat demanding he leave Walford for good? Carol starts to prepare for Billie's birthday homecoming

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alfie's world is blown apart after Michael works out Kat's secret. Glenda lets Carol know the truth about Lewis, and Billy does the dirty on Jean

programme.desc: The women hear the Red Cross has been sending parcels to the camp - so Rose, Blanche and Dorothy try to discover why they are not getting through to the inmates. World War Two drama, with Ann Bell, Patricia Lawrence and Louise Jameson

programme.sub-title: The Black Virgin of Vladimir - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. An American acquaintance of Lovejoy's is sold some fake antiques, so he sets out to exact revenge on the dealer responsible - and make a little money into the bargain. Linda Gray and Brian Blessed guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana investigate a spate of mysterious thefts at Bayview, and Tom ends up facing a shoplifting charge - but Diana decides to defend him

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly buys an old lawnmower from Auntie, which Compo and Clegg promptly help him to sell. Meanwhile, Marina generates gossip with her pastry purchase. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard hires a surveillance team to follow Pearl when his suspicions are aroused during a telephone conversation. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly investigates the case of a bus that disappeared between stops. Whimsical comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest must race to save Bunty from the hangman's noose when she is tried for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Emer Kenny

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: Mick and Shazia visit Cornwall's famous Towan Studios where some rare rock memorabilia has been stolen and the owner killed

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Brian spends time at home making peace with himself over his dismissal, he agrees to help Esther's friend Margaret find her missing brother Peter, in the interest of keeping his mind active. Peter was the accountant of a man who has gone to prison for the murder of his wife, so Brian visits the convict hoping he will be able to assist. However, his investigation begins to encroach on the Ucos team's efforts to track down a conman who may be connected to several outstanding cases. With Rosalind Ayres and Art Malik

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki travels to her childhood home in South Africa after being hired by a private security firm to investigate the disappearance of five young men in the 1980s. Meanwhile, Harry and Leo become embroiled in their efforts to reverse the deportation of a Zimbabwean asylum-seeker from the UK

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.sub-title: The Black Virgin of Vladimir - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. An American acquaintance of Lovejoy's is sold some fake antiques, so he sets out to exact revenge on the dealer responsible - and make a little money into the bargain. Linda Gray and Brian Blessed guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: A body is pulled out of the river near Harry's local pub, and something about the dead man feels familiar to the sleuth. Harry and Fergus's investigation takes in the house of the grieving widow, a Polish restaurant, a boarding school and a greyhound track as they seek to uncover the sinister truth. Crime drama, starring Jane Seymour

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The longer the search for Tim Tze's parents takes, the closer the bond becomes between the lad and his surrogate mentor PC Smith. Meanwhile, Deakin discovers the family's restaurant has been put up for sale and revelations about an extramarital affair come to light. Police drama, starring Shaun Scott, Alex Walkinshaw and Carl Tong

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Vivien is still troubled despite the end of the trial, but refuses Lily's offer of help. Heston agonises over the location for a date, and a man uses Jimmi to escape blame for an accident by accusing him of misdiagnosing an injured girl. Archie and Michelle meet a postgraduate student at an eco-fair

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A cafe owner makes his distaste for a woman breast-feeding on his premises vehemently known, and receives a stern warning from a group of women determined to change his ways. Heston offers Michelle and Ruth the use of his home in Tuscany for a holiday, and Melody becomes jealous when Archie receives a surprise visit from an attractive postgraduate

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mickie accidentally tells a woman she isn't pregnant when she is - a mistake that almost costs the patient her life in the operating theatre. Owen learns Lauren is seeking an injunction to prevent him seeing Katy, while Connie and Michael conspire to stop Ric getting his old job back so they can hire someone more accommodating to their plans. Tricia discovers Mark has nowhere to stay, and offers him the use of her spare room

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Linda struggles to incorporate the care of her sister's two children into her hectic schedule, but finds support from an unlikely source. Meanwhile, Lloyd tries to piece together the series of events that led to a woman with Alzheimer's being admitted, and Keith Parr returns to the ED with an injury that could have been sustained when Sam forcibly restrained him. Guest starring Marshall Lancaster, Jean Boht and Jayne Ashbourne

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The longer the search for Tim Tze's parents takes, the closer the bond becomes between the lad and his surrogate mentor PC Smith. Meanwhile, Deakin discovers the family's restaurant has been put up for sale and revelations about an extramarital affair come to light. Police drama, starring Shaun Scott, Alex Walkinshaw and Carl Tong

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat's doubts about Alfie lead her to Michael - but will she take him up on his offer? A letter from Billy's past has terrible consequences for Jay

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alfie decides to pull out all the stops for Kat, forcing her into a difficult position. Jay brings a surprise visitor to see Billy, and Carol finally lets go of Billie

programme.title: Tenko
programme.desc: Sally becomes increasingly reliant on Nellie as her pregnancy nears its end. Second World War drama, with Ann Bell, Renee Aherson and Louise Jameson

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Henry Crabbe prepares for his retirement and the opening of his restaurant - but the police force seems reluctant to let him go. First episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.sub-title: The Black Virgin of Vladimir - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Lovejoy sets out to exact revenge on the dealer responsible for deceiving his American acquaintance - and make a little money for himself into the bargain. Linda Gray and Brian Blessed guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom opts for a policy of complete honesty at all times, while Diana tries to cope with her niece, the 'Clapham Strangler'. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler goes on the run when he has a glass too much of Auntie's home-made wine, leaving Compo, Truly and Barry to track him down. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry comes to Marina's rescue after a nasty bike accident - only to spark rumours of marital disharmony. However, Truly is quick to suggest a remedy for the gossip with the help of borrowed lipstick. Comedy, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg knows he is in for a spot of bother when Howard tells him to expect a postal delivery he does not want Pearl to know about. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Catherine confronts Clare and presents Ryan with an ultimatum, while Faisal and Joanna hatch a plan that takes an unexpected turn. Crime drama by Sally Wainwright, starring Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of pornographer Jim Hockney is reopened when his daughter Sara provides DNA proof he wasn't her father. The team tries to track down jazz singer Angela Gold, who was the victim's girlfriend at the time and disappeared from the music scene after being acquitted of the crime, and it's Brian's replacement Danny Griffin (Nicholas Lyndhurst) who manages to find her. However, with Angela professing her innocence and Sara's mother Jane also arousing suspicion, it becomes a struggle to determine who is telling the truth. With a guest appearance by former Corrie killer Brian Capron

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo and Harry are called to investigate the unexpected deaths of three patients on the same ward at a London hospital, and are introduced to DC Julia Catina and DI Frank Skipper. Suspicion falls on a professor, but when he commits suicide, Nikki comes under pressure from his daughter to find a reason for his actions, and the pathologist soon makes a link to a case involving rape and murder

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 16-year-old case of an infamous getaway driver convicted of murdering his wife when new evidence suggests it could have been a miscarriage of justice. But Gerry, who was on the original investigation, refuses to believe the prisoner is anything but guilty. Meanwhile, Brian is in a reflective mood, convinced his days at Ucos are numbered after his actions against former police commander Embleton. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star as the veteran coppers, with Amanda Redman as their boss

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.sub-title: The Black Virgin of Vladimir - Part Two

programme.sub-title: An Unhappy Happy Is a Dangerous Thing
programme.desc: Harry and Fergus agree to help local loan shark Happy find out who murdered his employee in return for the ripping up Fergus's father's sizeable debt. However in doing so, Harry draws the attention of a fledgling serial killer. Crime drama, starring Jane Seymour and Rohan Nedd

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of Simon's journey along the northern tropic begins in Laos, where he takes a boat down the Mekong River, and in Vietnam he investigates the trafficking of young women into China. Simon then heads to Taiwan where he visits the typhoon-damaged central highlands and he concludes his trip in Hawaii, which he discovers may not be quite the paradise the tourist brochures claim

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of Simon's journey along the northern tropic begins in Laos, where he takes a boat down the Mekong River, and in Vietnam he investigates the trafficking of young women into China. Simon then heads to Taiwan where he visits the typhoon-damaged central highlands and he concludes his trip in Hawaii, which he discovers may not be quite the paradise the tourist brochures claim

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: The Green Green Grass

programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe's beloved cat goes missing, so she turns to Mr Humphries for support in her hour of need. Fearing the feline may never return, the staff buy her a kitten. John Inman and Mollie Sugden star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: When You Take a Good Bite, Yorkshire Tastes Terrible
programme.desc: When Clegg receives news of an old friend dying in America, Compo and Seymour join him on a journey down memory lane, recalling time spent at school walking on gates and climbing trees - forgetting, of course, that it was many years ago. programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivan's comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber

programme.desc: The staff at Grace Brothers are up in arms when it is decided that those who have reached a certain age are to be made redundant - leaving Mrs Slocombe facing the sack. They are far from appeased when they meet her successor. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden

programme.desc: Compo speculates why blood rushes to the head when upside down but not to the feet while standing up, so Seymour sets up an experiment to demonstrate the way the body works. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Michael Aldridge

programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Radcliffe and Edith Bowman put down their mics and battle it out to pick up some profit-making collectables and curios. programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy take a weekend hunting trip in the country. Back home, Saffy has a baby shower with her friends, but one of them develops an unhealthy fixation. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Emma Bunton and Clarissa Dickson Wright

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finds herself on the horns of a dilemma - Patsy is presenting the fashion event of the year at the Royal Albert Hall, but Saffy has gone unexpectedly into labour, leaving her mother torn between love and duty

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A new boyfriend boasting a selection of lost Beatles recordings makes Eddie think she's hit the big time, but her plans to marry him and invite an elite group of producers to hear the tapes are dashed in spectacular fashion. Elton John and Robert Lindsay guest star

programme.desc: Immunity is up for grabs, as well as a $10,000 prize! Plus, the winning contestant gets to take part in a cook-off against professional chef Sergio Perera

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christopher's needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Hugo and the Ice Cream Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets some newborn pups fighting to survive, and helps teach Hugo, a Cane Corso rescued from quarantine at Dover, how to be a dog

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. Now he is forced to sleep in a hospital bed in his kitchen - but it won't be for much longer, as his old workmates team up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to adapt his home to his needs, with designer Charlie Luxton on hand to keep the boys in check

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Orpington, south-east London, to meet hospital worker Jason Sulong, who was involved in a motorcycle accident that left him partially paralysed. Now he is unable to climb the stairs in his home and has difficulty accessing his bathroom and washing facilities, so designer Gabrielle Blackman teams up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to work to adapt his home to his needs over the course of just nine days

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A film crew comes to Button House to shoot a reconstruction of an assassination plot for a historical documentary - a scheme which may have involved one of the spirits. Mike persuades Alison to give an interview about the old house in the hope of drumming up some publicity, forcing her to turn to Thomas for advice on the art of public speaking

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House, with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is confused by his feelings for this strange arrival, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy is persuaded to allow Patsy and Eddie to babysit for the day, allowing her to have some romantic time with John - unaware the pair have an ulterior motive involving a fashion shoot and an outrageous designer. Fern Britton and Jean-Paul Gaultier guest star

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The first of the final two episodes of the sitcom sees the unlucky-in-love shop owner having received two proposals of marriage - one from dreamboat chef Gary and the other from new boyfriend Mike. Who will she choose, if anyone? And if she does make a decision, could she actually cope with the pressures of being a wife? Tom Ellis and Bo Poraj co-star as the rivals for her affections, with Patricia Hodge, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The very last episode of the hit sitcom sees the shopkeeper making one final attempt to accept herself and find contentment. Will she ever be truly happy? Miranda Hart stars, with Patricia Hodge, Tom Ellis, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips, plus guest appearances from Mark Heap, Liza Tarbuck and Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Basildon, Essex, to meet nine-year-old Brandon, who suffers a severe form of epilepsy and can have up to 30 seizures a day. He requires 24-hour care and mum Jenny can't let him out of her sight for a moment - and to make matters worse, the family home simply isn't safe enough for the youngster. Luckily, the local tradesmen have heard of the family's plight and arrive in force to help Nick and the team transform the home, with designer Naomi Cleaver on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Rufus Hound and Chris McCausland join antiques experts Margie Cooper and Ochuko Ojiri in Merseyside. There are lots of laughs and bags of buys, but will there be profits aplenty?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw start in south-west Wales before heading to Somerset for auction, but some fake currency and a leg of mutton could cause them problems

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The work of a pioneering veterinary practice, where 'Bionic Vet' Noel Fitzpatrick and a team of more than 100 surgeons, nurses, ward attendants and physiotherapists help some of the nation's hardest-to-cure pets through cutting-edge treatment and operations. This series opens with a special episode featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The series showcasing the extraordinary work of veterinary surgeon Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team at Fitzpatrick Referrals where families from all over the world seek hope and solutions for their animal friends. Two-year-old Boston terrier Rogan needs Noel's help with his debilitating hip dysplasia. Noel explains to Rogan's mums Megan and Shana that, due to Rogan's age and breed, an off-the-shelf cemented total hip replacement has an increased chance of loosening over time. Noel's offered alternative is a next-generation uncemented implant system, custom-made for Rogan. Handsome nine-year-old Hungarian vizsla Brodie is affected by developmental elbow disease. Noel finds that the joint has completely collapsed and is infected as the result of a previous botched operation by another vet. Poor Brodie is now in constant pain, but Noel is able to restore hope for Brodie and his family with a new customised implant system that will fuse the damaged bones together. And Vinnie the two-year-old tabby cat is rushed into the surgery after being hit by a car. Thankfully he has no internal injuries but he has sustained significant damage to his back legs. Noel and the team fight to repair the damage before infection can take hold

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Jools Holland and pop band the Divine Comedy guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and bizarre characters. Lauren fights with Kelly over Ryan, and 1950s landlady Ma Willow makes her first appearance

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike camp in the grounds while the house is being fumigated for woodworm, and become competitive over who is the better camper. Pat and the Captain embrace the chance to spend some time in the great outdoors again, but Mary is terrified that the 'woodworm men' intend to rid the house of its ghostly residents and resolves to scare them away

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before it's too late. programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy take a weekend hunting trip in the country. Back home, Saffy has a baby shower with her friends, but one of them develops an unhealthy fixation. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Emma Bunton and Clarissa Dickson Wright

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finds herself on the horns of a dilemma - Patsy is presenting the fashion event of the year at the Royal Albert Hall, but Saffy has gone unexpectedly into labour, leaving her mother torn between love and duty

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A new boyfriend boasting a selection of lost Beatles recordings makes Eddie think she's hit the big time, but her plans to marry him and invite an elite group of producers to hear the tapes are dashed in spectacular fashion. Elton John and Robert Lindsay guest star

programme.desc: Immunity is up for grabs, as well as a $10,000 prize! Plus, the winning contestant gets to take part in a cook-off against professional chef Sergio Perera

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christopher's needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The first of the final two episodes of the sitcom sees the unlucky-in-love shop owner having received two proposals of marriage - one from dreamboat chef Gary and the other from new boyfriend Mike. programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The very last episode of the hit sitcom sees the shopkeeper making one final attempt to accept herself and find contentment. Will she ever be truly happy? programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A film crew comes to Button House to shoot a reconstruction of an assassination plot for a historical documentary - a scheme which may have involved one of the spirits. Mike persuades Alison to give an interview about the old house in the hope of drumming up some publicity, forcing her to turn to Thomas for advice on the art of public speaking

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House, with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is confused by his feelings for this strange arrival, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren receives a proposal, and Bernie's sisters make an appearance, while Derek Faye meets Bonnie Langford, guest starring as herself

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting and the Death Cleaners help with her fear of discussing 'the end'

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: A detailed look at the life of Aaron Hernandez, a millionaire athlete with a fiancee and baby, who was shockingly convicted of murder

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer leaves his comfort zone as he attempts to set a world speed record for a hovercraft. These machines are notoriously difficult to control, so to learn the basic skills needed he's put through his paces by Royal Marines in a military training operation. To achieve his goal, Guy will need to stay over 86mph for a kilometre, but at these speeds, because of the hovercraft's inherent instability, even the most experienced pilots can lose control - and the worsening weather makes things much more challenging

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike fan takes on the challenge of building the world's fastest soapbox racer in an attempt to set a new speed record using just the pull of gravity and a tarmac road. To get to grips with going blisteringly fast on a public road, Guy tries his hand at street luge with former global champion Helene Schmit, before joining his team of engineers to explore the science of rolling resistance and the importance of tyre pressure. Armed with what they believe to be the perfect formula, they produce a unique soapbox cart inspired by Mother Nature and, after training with the Team GB women's bobsled pair, confidence is high - but are disaster and mayhem just around the corner?

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a rare Ford Zodiac Farnham Estate, while Dave heads for an isolated tent in the Midlands to collect a Triumph Toledo

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Guided missiles, the Wasserfall, and the war-changing V2 were dreamed up and tested in Peenemunde, a German seaside city suddenly overtaken by Nazi elite

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Brighton College, where objects up for valuation include a medal awarded to a teenage veteran of the Battle of Trafalgar, a silver box given to a family by US president John F Kennedy shortly before his assassination, and one of the largest, rarest and most valuable pieces of Clarice Cliff pottery ever seen on the programme

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Brighton College, where objects up for valuation include a cup and saucer purchased at a car boot sale, a portrait of a pig by a noted artist, and a collection of items found in a safe that once belonged to Agatha Christie

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A rusty 1970 Fiat 500 goes under the hammer - Derek Mathewson reckons it's the kind of vehicle that is so iconic that it has to be saved

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike fan takes on the challenge of building the world's fastest soapbox racer in an attempt to set a new speed record using just the pull of gravity and a tarmac road. To get to grips with going blisteringly fast on a public road, Guy tries his hand at street luge with former global champion Helene Schmit, before joining his team of engineers to explore the science of rolling resistance and the importance of tyre pressure. Armed with what they believe to be the perfect formula, they produce a unique soapbox cart inspired by Mother Nature and, after training with the Team GB women's bobsled pair, confidence is high - but are disaster and mayhem just around the corner?

Liz still doesn't dare to trust her colleagues and decides to take matters into her own hands - with surprising consequences as she makes an alarming discovery that brings her closer to the gangster Crowther

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison is moved to a safe house with Julie while the police search for the gang, but she is kidnapped by Braden and used as leverage to demand that Liam return the necklace. This results in a tense warehouse stand-off, and Alison is in a desperate situation as armed forces storm the building. Rose Ayling-Ellis stars

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Kirsten uncovers evidence suggesting the murders were part of a coordinated attack. Her investigation takes her into the heart of Scottish politics, and the Naval cover up of a near catastrophe in the US. Amy starts to question who she can trust on Vigil, when a message from Kirsten makes Amy realise she is being deliberately undermined

programme.title: Call the Cleaners
programme.desc: Cameras follow mother and daughter Maxine and Jasmine as they face one of their biggest hoarder jobs yet - an elderly man who has a bungalow packed with 30 years' worth of belongings. Meanwhile, George and his team face a difficult clean up after a body was discovered in a flat

programme.title: The Crow Girl
programme.desc: Jeanette and Sophia dig into Victoria's past as she and Madeleine plan their future. Amar takes fate into his own hands, and Jeanette uncovers a secret that changes everything

programme.title: Tour de Fred
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix heads to the Republic of Ireland to take on a series of cycling challenges, beginning on the sometimes gruelling but often spectacular roads of Co Wexford. He climbs to Slieve Coillte, the highest point on the Hook peninsula, and also steps aboard the Dunbrody Famine Ship and visits the world's oldest working lighthouse

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the popular medical drama set in the emergency ward of a Chicago hospital, charting the everyday hassles and heroism of its staff. Dr Mark Greene wrangles with a big career decision, while surgeon Peter Benton gets into hot water after performing an unauthorised procedure. Medical drama, starring Anthony Edwards and Eriq La Salle

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The duo are sucked into the fast and furious world of Formula 1 after Bath's famous motorsport dynasty, the Addingtons, report that their up-and-coming driver has died during a sub-3.5-second pit stop. As an F1 fan herself, McDonald won't let patriarch and Team Coach Archie Addington run rings around her, but when a significant someone from her past gets dragged into the investigation, she's thrown off course. But it's one of Dodds' specialisms that leads them to uncover the darker side of success and finally unravel the truth

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam's mental state declines severely when fact and fiction collide, a suspicious Maria is stood up by Gary, Debbie's pals organise a surprise holiday, and Carla makes a discovery

programme.title: Lucy Letby: Beyond Reasonable Doubt?
programme.desc: Anouk Curry reports on the conviction of the former nurse, who was found guilty of killing seven newborn babies and attempting to kill seven others in one of the most shocking murder cases in British history. This programme explores the views of a team of international scientists who say the prosecution case simply doesn't stand up to scrutiny. So is she guilty beyond reasonable doubt or could she be a victim of one of Britain's worst miscarriages of justice

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Glen Powell who is appearing in dystopian action thriller The Running Man. Also on the sofa are Oscar-winner Michelle Yeoh, who is playing Madame Morrible in Wicked: For Good, Emmy-winning Brit Rosamund Pike, currently in crime caper sequel Now You See Me: Now You Don't and comedian-actor Jack Whitehall, one of the stars of tense thriller series Malice. Plus, Ed Sheeran chats and performs Camera, from his current album Play

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Food poisoning victims flood into the emergency room, while Lewis argues with Cvetic regarding a senile man, and Ross helps Greene care for a family devastated by a drunk driver's actions. Medical drama, starring Sherry Stringfield, George Clooney and Anthony Edwards

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi sets out to reverse the fortunes of the Grove Arms in Wiltshire. Three years ago, Nina Bartlett gave up a successful legal career in the City to fulfil her dream of running a dog-friendly village pub. However, despite her best efforts and last year's good turnover, the Grove in Ludwell is currently making a loss, leaving her unsure which way to turn. Nina admits she is afraid to rely on her own staff, which leaves her doing the bulk of the work herself and on the brink of burnout. Alex takes Nina a lesson in getting the basics right, but does the former law specialist have the energy to see this through?
